text:
Sure, here's a short story:

Once upon an age when magic soared through the whispers of ancient forests and beneath the moonlit sky, there lived a young sorcerer named Elara. In her cottage by the brook—where the scent of wildflowers wafted through the air and the river sang its ancient secrets to the rolling hills—Elara practiced her spells under the watchful stars.

Her first lesson came from a friendly-looking toad named Toadly who'd appeared the night before. Toadly, whom Elara named because she always seemed to offer wisdom from the periphery, had hatched out of an egg left behind by another world amidst one of Elara's many experiments. He introduced her to the basics: the creation of crystals that refracted light like diamonds and the soothing power of a calming balm made of moon-dew and night-shade petals.

One evening, as a full moon cast its ethereal glow through the window, Elara was immersed
